prevalence.source: Orphanet; amyloidosis epidemiology reviews; Indian neurology/cardiology center reports
prevalence.trend: increasing diagnosed prevalence due to improved awareness and access to genetics

burden.dalyPerCapita: very low (rare) but high per-patient morbidity and mortality without treatment
burden.mortalityRate: Meaningful without disease-modifying therapy; reduced with early diagnosis and treatment
burden.source: Registry outcomes and modeled cardiomyopathy/neuropathy burden context

riskFactors: delayed diagnosis due to nonspecific symptoms
vulnerablePopulations: patients with cardiomyopathy/HFpEF plus red-flag features
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical suspicion plus genetic confirmation (TTR); tissue biopsy with amyloid typing when needed; cardiac imaging and scintigraphy where available

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no population screening; cascade family testing when feasible
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; limited by access to high-cost therapies and specialist care

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Diagnosis concentrated in tertiary centers; genetics and scintigraphy access limited outside metros
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Disease-modifying therapy when accessible (TTR stabilizers/silencers), symptomatic neuropathy and heart failure management, genetic counseling and cascade testing
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Underdiagnosis, high out-of-pocket costs for diagnostics/therapies, limited specialist density
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Improving awareness among cardiology/neurology subspecialists and growing genetics capacity
